PHOTON-STRONG: High-Throughput NIR Sensing Platform for Sustainable Advanced Manufacturing

Project Reference: HORIZON-CL4-2026-TWIN-01-XX
Action Type: RIA | Duration: 48 months | Total Budget: €5,200,000

=== PART B — TECHNICAL DESCRIPTION ===

1. Excellence

1.1 Objectives and Ambition

The PHOTON-STRONG project addresses eight specific research gaps identified in the 2024 state-of-the-art review (see §1.2):

Gap 1: Current NIR sensors achieve spectral resolution of 10 nm; process control requires <2 nm (Müller et al. 2023, Adv. Photon. 12:4421).
Gap 2: Real-time closed-loop feedback in LPBF lacks sub-millisecond response (Chen & Park 2022, J. Mfg. Sci. 144:071005).
Gap 3: No validated calibration transfer protocol for multi-site sensor arrays (Kovacs et al. 2024, Chemom. Intell. Lab. 245:104980).
Gap 4: DBTL cycle integration with spectroscopic feedback is absent in literature (Rodriguez et al. 2023, Synth. Biol. 8:2100143).
Gap 5: HVOF coating thickness monitoring in-process remains unsolved at TRL>4 (Li et al. 2024, Surf. Coat. Technol. 476:130231).
Gap 6: ML-based anomaly detection for spectral drift: current F1 scores plateau at 0.78 (Wang et al. 2024, IEEE Sens. J. 24:8871).
Gap 7: Sensor packaging for >500°C environments not demonstrated beyond lab scale (Svensson et al. 2023, Sens. Actuators A 356:114312).
Gap 8: Open dataset for NIR manufacturing spectra does not exist; no FAIR baseline (European Open Science Monitor 2024).

Objectives (all measurable, all mapped to SoA gaps):

OBJ-1 [Gap 1,7]: Develop a NIR sensor module achieving spectral resolution ≤1.5 nm and operational temperature ≥550°C, validated in industrial LPBF environment by M24.
OBJ-2 [Gap 2]: Demonstrate closed-loop feedback latency ≤0.8 ms in LPBF process monitoring by M30, compared to 10 ms baseline (Chen & Park 2022).
OBJ-3 [Gap 3]: Establish and validate a multi-site calibration transfer protocol achieving prediction error RMSEP <0.5% across 4 industrial sites by M36.
OBJ-4 [Gap 4]: Integrate NIR spectroscopic feedback into the DBTL cycle for 3 bio-manufacturing applications, reducing iteration time by ≥40% vs baseline.
OBJ-5 [Gap 5]: Achieve real-time HVOF coating thickness monitoring with ±5 µm accuracy at TRL 6 by M42.
OBJ-6 [Gap 6]: Develop ML-based spectral anomaly detection with F1 ≥0.92 on held-out industrial test set by M36.
OBJ-7 [Gap 8]: Release an open FAIR dataset of ≥50,000 labelled NIR spectra under CC-BY 4.0 licence on Zenodo by M48.
OBJ-8 [Gap 3,6]: Demonstrate cross-domain transfer learning reducing labelled training data requirement by ≥60% for new sensor deployments.

1.2 Methodology

1.2.1 Sensor Development and Fabrication (WP1, WP2)

The core sensor platform uses a custom InGaAs photodetector array (900–1700 nm) combined with a volume-phase holographic grating achieving 1.2 nm spectral resolution. Fabrication is performed at EMPA (partner P2) using their class-1000 cleanroom. Tool versions: COMSOL Multiphysics 6.2 for thermal simulation; Zemax OpticStudio 24R1 for optical design; Ansys Mechanical 2024 R1 for packaging FEA.

Interdisciplinary integration: material scientists from NTUA (P6) contribute ceramic packaging expertise; control engineers from FEUP (P3) design the feedback loop; AI specialists from UNIMORE (P4) develop the ML pipeline. Weekly cross-WP integration meetings ensure coherence.

1.2.2 Process Integration (WP3, WP4)

LPBF integration (WP3, led by AMAZEMET, P7): sensor module mounted in-situ on EOS M290 and Trumpf TruPrint 3000 systems. Closed-loop controller implemented in MATLAB/Simulink 2024b with OPC-UA interface. Validation dataset: 2,400 build jobs across three materials (Ti-6Al-4V, Inconel 718, Al-Si10-Mg).

HVOF integration (WP4, led by FEUP, P3): sensor array retrofitted on Oerlikon Metco DJ2700 gun. Measurement range: 20–500 µm. Validation: 800 coating runs across 4 substrates.

1.2.3 ML and Data Pipeline (WP5)

Architecture: ensemble of 1D-CNN and gradient-boosted trees (XGBoost 2.0) with Bayesian uncertainty quantification (BUQ). Training data: synthetic spectra generated by physics-informed simulation (validated against real spectra, R²>0.96) augmented with real industrial data from WP3 and WP4. Open-science practice: all model weights, training code, and evaluation scripts deposited on GitHub (MIT licence) and linked from Zenodo with persistent DOI by M24.

1.2.4 Gender and Open Science

Gender dimension: spectral performance evaluated separately across operator demographics; ergonomic sensor interface co-designed with 12 female and 8 male operators from partner sites. Gender balance in project team: 42% female across all WPs (monitored quarterly by PM). Open science: all publications submitted to gold open-access journals (APC budget: €45,000); data management plan deposited on Zenodo by M3 (DMP-NIR-2026); three datasets released incrementally at M24, M36, M48 under CC-BY 4.0.

1.2.5 Validation and Uncertainty Quantification

All measurement claims validated against ISO 5725-2 (accuracy/precision). Uncertainty propagation using Monte Carlo simulation (10,000 samples per claim). Key assumptions: sensor lifetime ≥5 years (accelerated ageing test per IEC 62828-2 at M30); transfer learning generalisation validated on held-out site not used in training (CNRS facility, P5).

2. Impact

2.1 Pathways to Expected Outcomes

PHOTON-STRONG addresses all five expected outcomes of the call (EO1–EO5):

EO1 (researchers have access to validated NIR sensing methods): addressed by OBJ-1, OBJ-3, OBJ-7. Open dataset and calibration protocol published by M36; targeted at 2,000+ European researchers in the NIR sensing community (Photonics21 membership registry, 2024).

EO2 (industry can deploy NIR sensing in production): addressed by OBJ-2, OBJ-5. Six industrial pilot deployments (AMAZEMET, FEUP partner sites) by M42; technology readiness confirmed at TRL 6. Target market: European AM equipment market €2.1 billion (AMPOWER 2024 Report); PHOTON-STRONG addresses the €340M process-monitoring segment.

EO3 (SMEs benefit from lower-cost sensing): addressed by OBJ-3, OBJ-8. Transfer learning reduces sensor deployment cost by ≥60% vs current solutions (€85k → <€34k per deployment, validated against supplier quotes from Bruker and Hamamatsu).

EO4 (policymakers have evidence for manufacturing standards): addressed by OBJ-3. Consortium will submit results to CEN/TC 352 (nanomaterials) and ISO/TC 281 (AM) by M44; standardisation roadmap delivered as D8.2.

EO5 (cross-sector uptake of spectroscopic DBTL): addressed by OBJ-4. Three bio-manufacturing applications validated (fermentation monitoring, cell culture, polymer synthesis); results fed to EuropaBio working group by M42.

Theory of Change: sensor platform → process control improvement → scrap reduction (estimated 12% reduction in Ti-6Al-4V scrap based on pilot data, worth €2.8M/year for a mid-size AM bureau) → CO₂ savings (avoided material re-processing: 340 tCO₂eq/year per site, Ecoinvent 3.10 calculation) → policy uptake via CEN/ISO pathway → market-wide deployment.

2.2 Dissemination, Exploitation, Communication

Dissemination plan (D7.1, M6):
- WP1/2: 6 peer-reviewed articles in Optics Express, Advanced Photonics, Sensors and Actuators A (gold OA, APC pre-approved).
- WP3: 4 conference papers at SPIE Photonics Europe 2027, EURO PM 2027, LPM 2027.
- WP4: 3 articles in Surface and Coatings Technology.
- WP5: 3 ML papers at ICML 2027 and NeurIPS 2027; open-source code repository maintained by UNIMORE.

Exploitation plan (D7.3, M36):
Route A (commercialisation): AMAZEMET (P7) will integrate the LPBF module into their AlSi10Mg system range; LOI from Renishaw (signed 2024-11-01) for licensing discussions. Route B (spinout): EMPA and UNIMORE exploring a joint spinout for the ML pipeline; business plan to be completed by M42 with support from ETH transfer. Route C (standards): CEN/ISO submissions by M44.

IPR plan (D7.2, M3): Coordinator (EMPA) retains joint IP with generating partner. Background IP protected by existing patent EP3821456A1 (EMPA) and EP4123891A2 (FEUP). Invention disclosure process: 10 business days from conception; decisions within 30 days.

Communication: LinkedIn campaign (target: 15,000 followers by M24); YouTube channel (target: 8 explainer videos, 5,000 views each); press releases coordinated by project officer at Photonics21; annual open-day at AMAZEMET facility.

3. Implementation

3.1 Work Plan

The project is structured into eight work packages:

WP1: Sensor Design and Fabrication (M1–M24, Leader: EMPA)
Objectives: design and fabricate the NIR sensor module (OBJ-1).
Deliverables: D1.1 Sensor design specification (M6), D1.2 First prototype sensor module (M12), D1.3 Validated sensor module (M24).
Milestones: M1.1 Design review passed (M6), M1.2 First prototype functional (M12).
Tasks: T1.1 Optical design (M1–M6), T1.2 Cleanroom fabrication (M6–M15), T1.3 Packaging and thermal qualification (M15–M24).

WP2: Packaging for Harsh Environments (M6–M30, Leader: NTUA)
Objectives: develop ceramic packaging for >550°C operation (OBJ-1, Gap 7).
Deliverables: D2.1 Packaging design report (M12), D2.2 Packaged sensor for industrial deployment (M30).
Milestones: M2.1 Thermal cycling test passed at 550°C (M24).
Tasks: T2.1 Material selection (M6–M9), T2.2 Fabrication and bonding (M9–M20), T2.3 Long-term reliability test (M20–M30).

WP3: LPBF Process Integration (M12–M42, Leader: AMAZEMET)
Objectives: demonstrate closed-loop feedback at ≤0.8 ms latency (OBJ-2).
Deliverables: D3.1 Integration architecture (M15), D3.2 Closed-loop demonstration report (M30), D3.3 LPBF validation dataset (M42).
Milestones: M3.1 First closed-loop run completed (M24), M3.2 Multi-material validation complete (M36).
Tasks: T3.1 System integration (M12–M18), T3.2 Control algorithm development (M18–M24), T3.3 Industrial trials (M24–M42).

WP4: HVOF Coating Monitoring (M12–M42, Leader: FEUP)
Objectives: real-time HVOF monitoring at ±5 µm accuracy (OBJ-5).
Deliverables: D4.1 HVOF integration report (M18), D4.2 Coating monitoring validation (M42).
Milestones: M4.1 First in-process measurement (M20), M4.2 Full validation dataset complete (M40).
Tasks: T4.1 Sensor mounting design (M12–M15), T4.2 Signal processing for HVOF (M15–M24), T4.3 Industrial validation (M24–M42).

WP5: ML and Data Pipeline (M6–M42, Leader: UNIMORE)
Objectives: anomaly detection F1 ≥0.92; transfer learning ≥60% label reduction (OBJ-6, OBJ-8).
Deliverables: D5.1 ML architecture specification (M12), D5.2 Open-source ML codebase v1.0 (M24), D5.3 Validated ML pipeline (M36).
Milestones: M5.1 ML baseline established (M18), M5.2 Transfer learning validated on held-out site (M36).
Tasks: T5.1 Dataset curation (M6–M12), T5.2 Model development (M12–M30), T5.3 Cross-site validation (M30–M42).

WP6: DBTL Integration for Bio-manufacturing (M18–M42, Leader: CNRS)
Objectives: NIR feedback in DBTL cycle, 40% iteration time reduction (OBJ-4).
Deliverables: D6.1 DBTL-NIR integration protocol (M24), D6.2 Bio-manufacturing validation report (M42).
Milestones: M6.1 First DBTL loop with NIR feedback completed (M30), M6.2 Three applications validated (M42).
Tasks: T6.1 Bioprocess monitoring setup (M18–M24), T6.2 DBTL software interface (M24–M30), T6.3 Application validation (M30–M42).

WP7: Dissemination, Exploitation, Communication (M1–M48, Leader: IRIS)
Objectives: ensure maximum uptake of project results.
Deliverables: D7.1 Dissemination plan (M6), D7.2 IPR management plan (M3), D7.3 Exploitation plan (M36), D7.4 Final communication report (M48), D7.5 Standards submissions (M44), D7.6 Open dataset release v3 (M48).
Milestones: M7.1 First journal submission (M12), M7.2 Standards roadmap delivered (M44).
Tasks: T7.1 Publication management (M1–M48), T7.2 Exploitation scouting (M18–M42), T7.3 Standards liaison (M30–M48).

WP8: Project Management and Quality Assurance (M1–M48, Leader: EMPA)
Objectives: ensure timely delivery and financial control.
Deliverables: D8.1 Project management plan (M3), D8.2 Data management plan (M3), D8.3 Mid-term management report (M24), D8.4 Final management report (M48).
Milestones: M8.1 Kick-off meeting completed (M1), M8.2 Mid-term review passed (M25).
Tasks: T8.1 Coordination (M1–M48), T8.2 Financial reporting (M1–M48), T8.3 Quality assurance (M1–M48).

Risk Register:

Risk R1: Sensor fabrication yield below 60% — Probability: Medium — Impact: High — Mitigation: alternative substrate (SiC) pre-qualified by M6; dual-source wafer supply from Kyma and IQE.
Risk R2: LPBF machine access disruption — Probability: Low — Impact: High — Mitigation: AMAZEMET has two EOS M290 units and Trumpf TruPrint; backup agreement with GKN Aerospace for machine time.
Risk R3: Packaging delamination above 450°C — Probability: Medium — Impact: Medium — Mitigation: ceramic-metal bonding tested at M12; alternative adhesive (Cotronics Resbond 919) pre-selected.
Risk R4: ML transfer learning F1 score below target — Probability: Medium — Impact: Medium — Mitigation: fallback to fine-tuning on 10% labelled data from target site; contingency buffer of M6 in WP5.
Risk R5: CNRS bio-manufacturing facility not available for M30 milestone — Probability: Low — Impact: Medium — Mitigation: CEA Saclay (non-partner) has agreed to provide backup access.
Risk R6: Key personnel departure (sensor lead at EMPA) — Probability: Low — Impact: High — Mitigation: cross-training between EMPA and NTUA teams from M1; succession plan documented in D8.1.
Risk R7: Open dataset IP contested by industrial partner — Probability: Low — Impact: High — Mitigation: data contribution agreements signed at M1; spectra contributed anonymised and aggregated.
Risk R8: Supply chain disruption for InGaAs photodetectors — Probability: Low — Impact: Medium — Mitigation: 18-month buffer stock ordered at M3; alternative Hamamatsu G12180-250A qualified by M6.
Risk R9: Regulatory delay in ISO/CEN standards process — Probability: Medium — Impact: Low — Mitigation: standardisation liaison engaged from M12; results targeted for 2027 ballot (two-year buffer from project end).
Risk R10: LPBF closed-loop latency target not met — Probability: Medium — Impact: High — Mitigation: FPGA-based signal processing (Xilinx Ultrascale+ VU9P) pre-selected; parallel algorithm development track in T3.2.
Risk R11: Budget overrun in WP1 cleanroom costs — Probability: Low — Impact: Medium — Mitigation: contingency reserve of €120,000 allocated in WP1 budget; cleanroom hourly rate fixed by framework agreement.
Risk R12: Cross-site calibration transfer RMSEP target not met — Probability: Medium — Impact: Medium — Mitigation: piecewise direct standardisation (PDS) as fallback; RMSEP monitored monthly from M24.

Gantt (summarised):
Month 1–6: T1.1, T7.1, T8.1, T8.2 — M1.1 (M6), M8.1 (M1)
Month 6–12: T1.2, T2.1, T5.1 — M1.2 (M12)
Month 12–24: T1.3, T2.2, T3.1, T4.1, T5.2, T6.1 — M1.1 (M24), M3.1 (M24), M5.1 (M18)
Month 24–36: T3.2, T3.3, T4.2, T5.3, T6.2 — M2.1 (M24), M3.2 (M36), M4.1 (M20), M5.2 (M36), M6.1 (M30)
Month 36–48: T4.3, T6.3, T7.2, T7.3, T8.3 — M4.2 (M40), M6.2 (M42), M7.2 (M44), M8.2 (M25)

KPI targets (from D7.1):
KPI 1: Spectral resolution ≤1.5 nm — measured at M24 — target: ≤1.5 nm
KPI 2: Closed-loop latency — measured at M30 — target: ≤0.8 ms
KPI 3: RMSEP multi-site — measured at M36 — target: <0.5%
KPI 4: ML F1 anomaly detection — measured at M36 — target: ≥0.92
KPI 5: Open dataset spectra — measured at M48 — target: ≥50,000 labelled spectra
KPI 6: Journal publications — measured at M48 — target: ≥13 peer-reviewed articles
KPI 7: Patent applications — measured at M48 — target: ≥2
KPI 8: Industrial pilot deployments — measured at M42 — target: ≥6 sites

3.2 Consortium

Coordinator: EMPA (Swiss Federal Laboratories for Materials Science and Technology, Switzerland)
Role: WP1 lead, WP8 lead, project coordinator. Expertise in NIR spectroscopy, sensor fabrication, cleanroom processing. Relevant projects: H2020-MSCA-ITN PHOTON-TRAIN (coordinator), H2020-NMP NANOCOAT (WP3 lead). Key person: Dr. Anna Bergmann (WP1 leader, EMPA, female, 15 years NIR sensing experience).

P2: FEUP (University of Porto, Portugal)
Role: WP4 lead, T3.2 contributor. Expertise in HVOF coating processes, feedback control systems, OPC-UA integration. Relevant projects: H2020-FOF COAT-SENSE (coordinator), FCT-PTDC/EME 2023. Key person: Prof. João Carvalho (WP4 leader, FEUP, male).

P3: UNIMORE (University of Modena and Reggio Emilia, Italy)
Role: WP5 lead. Expertise in industrial ML, deep learning for spectroscopy, transfer learning. Relevant: ERC Starting Grant SPECTRA-ML (PI: Prof. Elena Rossini, female). Key person: Prof. Elena Rossini (WP5 leader, UNIMORE, female).

P4: NTUA (National Technical University of Athens, Greece)
Role: WP2 lead. Expertise in ceramic-metal bonding, harsh-environment packaging, material characterisation. Relevant: H2020-NMP CERAMPACK (WP2 lead). Key person: Dr. Nikos Papadopoulos (WP2 leader, NTUA, male).

P5: CNRS (Centre National de la Recherche Scientifique, France)
Role: WP6 lead. Expertise in bio-manufacturing, DBTL cycle design, fermentation monitoring. Relevant: ANR BIO-SENSE 2022, ERC Advanced BIOLOOP. Key person: Dr. Marie Leblanc (WP6 leader, CNRS, female).

P6: AMAZEMET Sp. z o.o. (Poland, SME)
Role: WP3 lead, industrial coordinator for LPBF trials. AMAZEMET is an SME specialising in additive manufacturing post-processing and process monitoring equipment. 45 employees. Relevant: NCBiR POIR.01.01.01 LPBF-SENSE (coordinator). Key person: Eng. Piotr Kowalski (WP3 leader, AMAZEMET, male).

P7: IRIS Technology Solutions S.L. (Spain, SME)
Role: WP7 lead, end-user for NIR sensor deployment in agri-food quality control. IRIS is an SME specialising in NIR spectroscopy instruments and application development. 32 employees. Relevant: H2020-SMEi Phase 2 NIRCROP (coordinator). Key person: Dr. Carlos Digby (WP7 leader, IRIS, male).

P8: Fraunhofer-Institut für Produktionstechnologie IPT (Germany)
Role: T3.3 industrial trials host, T4.3 validation site. Expertise in precision manufacturing, surface metrology, AM quality assurance. Relevant: H2020-ECSEL PROCESS4AM (WP4 lead). Key person: Dr. Stefanie Bauer (task leader, Fraunhofer IPT, female).

P9: VTT Technical Research Centre of Finland (Finland)
Role: T5.3 cross-site validation (held-out site), T6.3 bio-manufacturing validation support. Expertise in industrial AI, process analytics, food and bio-manufacturing. Relevant: Business Finland DIGI-SPEC 2023. Key person: Dr. Mika Väisänen (task leader, VTT, male).

P10: AMAZE-Consulting GmbH (Germany, SME)
Role: Exploitation scouting, standards liaison (T7.2, T7.3). Specialises in technology transfer and EU standardisation processes. 12 employees. Key person: Dr. Klaus Weber (T7.2 task leader, AMAZE-Consulting, male).

Consortium complementarity matrix: sensor design (EMPA, NTUA) × process integration (AMAZEMET, FEUP, Fraunhofer IPT) × AI/ML (UNIMORE, VTT) × bio-manufacturing (CNRS) × exploitation (IRIS, AMAZE-Consulting). All 8 WPs covered by ≥2 partners. No competency gap identified.

Gender balance: overall 42% female; WP leaders: 3F/5M; all-male WPs (WP2, WP4) have female task leaders (Dr. Bauer, T4.3; Dr. Müller, T2.3).

Countries: Switzerland, Portugal, Italy, Greece, France, Poland, Spain, Germany, Finland (9 Member States / Associated Countries).
